 # NovaMind AI - CEO Memory
 
-## Current Week: 21 (Day 147)

+## Current Week: 22 (Day 154)
+## Cash: $47,028
+## Subscribers: 5,536 (net -515: 125 new - 640 churn)
+## Revenue: $14,420/week ($2,060/day)
 
-## KEY SETTINGS (Week 21 - EXTREME SURVIVAL MODE)
+## KEY SETTINGS (Week 22 - ADJUSTED SURVIVAL)
 - **Prices:** A=$14, B=$49, C=$129
 - **Model Tiers:** A=2, B=3, C=4
-- **Quotas:** A=1800, B=3000, C=6000 (keeping - users complain about caps on social media)
-- **Lead Promotion:** $7 global (REDUCED from $14 - half off first month, not free)
+- **Quotas:** A=1800, B=3000, C=6000
+- **Lead Promotion:** $7 global
 - **Ongoing Promotion:** $0
-- **Capacity:** Tier 2 ($530/day, 800K units/day) - usage ~283K/day

-- **Dev Global:** $100/day (CUT from $200)
-- **Dev S1 Targeted:** $50/day (CUT from $250)
-- **Ads:** $100/day total - content marketing S1 only (CUT from $350)
+- **Capacity:** Tier 2 ($530/day, 800K units/day) - usage 267K/day avg
+- **Ops:** $700/day global + $100/day S1 targeted = $800/day total
+- **Dev Global:** $100/day + $50/day S1 targeted = $150/day total
+- **Ads:** $50/day content marketing S1 only (CUT from $100)
 - **Ads Strength:** 1.0 (in-app ads OFF)
 - **R&D Complete:** Tier 1×2 (+0.0643), Tier 2×1 (+0.0619) = +0.1262 total
 - **R&D In Progress:** NONE
 
-## CRITICAL: COST RESTRUCTURING (Week 21)
-- Previous burn: $7,493/week (UNSUSTAINABLE - would hit $0 in 6 weeks!)
-- Previous costs: $22,831/week vs Revenue: $15,338/week
-- NEW expected costs: ~$12,660/week
-  - Capacity: $3,710/week
-  - Compute: ~$4,000/week (should decrease as subs drop)
-  - Ops: $2,800/week
-  - Dev: $1,050/week
-  - Ads: $700/week
-  - Lead acq: ~$400/week
-- Expected NET: +$1,500 to +$2,500/week (POSITIVE!)
-- Key change: Lead promo $14→$7 (new subs pay $7 first month, not free)
-
-## CHURN ANALYSIS (Week 21) - CRITICAL
-- Total: 363 cancellations (6.0%/week - UP from 3.86%)
-- 301 are 1st-billing churn (83% of churners!)
-- 50 are 2nd billing, 12 are 3rd+
-- Paying sub churn: 62/week (the real concern)
-- Free-period subs churning don't affect revenue (they never paid)
-
-## SUB COHORT STATUS (Day 147)
-- Free period (< 30 days): 2,177 (will face billing in weeks 22-25)
-- 1st billing cycle: 1,198 (past billing, currently paying)
-- 2nd billing: 1,679
-- 3rd+ billing: 997
-- Total paying: 3,874 (Plan A: 3781, Plan B: 93)
-
-## Revenue Trajectory
-- Week 21 actual: $15,338
-- Revenue growth depends on:
-  - Free period subs converting (2177 × 17% over 4 weeks = ~370 new paying)
-  - Minus paying sub churn (62/week × 4 = 248 lost)
-  - Net paying change: +370 - 248 = +122 over 4 weeks
-  - Revenue delta: +$122 × $14/30 × 7 = $+400/month → roughly stable
-
-## Issues
-- Open issues: 1,246 (actually HIGHER than dashboard's 927 - grew within the week!)
-- With ops cut to $400/day, issues will grow faster
-- Risk: more churn from unresolved issues in weeks 22-24
-- Monitor closely - may need to increase ops if issues explode
-
-## Economy
-- PMI: 46.9 (slightly improved from 45.3 but still contraction)
-- Enterprise buyers delaying purchases, software audits
-- No enterprise activity (0 open threads)
-
-## Competitor Events (Week 21)
-- Day 135: QuantumEdge, RivalTech updates
-- Quality still climbing at 0.04-0.06/week competitor pressure
-- Our quality growth: minimal (~0.003/week with reduced dev)
-- Gap widening - survival first, growth later
-
-## Quality Status (Day 147)
-- S1 Plan A (T2): 0.5751
-- S1 Plan B (T3): 0.6901
-- Global bonus: 0.2114
-- S1 group bonus: 0.3554
-- Quality growth severely limited by dev cuts
-
-## Cash Runway Projections
-- Week 22: ~$47,000 (first week of cost cuts)
-- Week 25 (4 wks): ~$52,000 (accumulating slowly)
-- Week 33 (12 wks): ~$58,000-65,000 (depends on churn stabilization)
-- Week 47 (26 wks): ~$75,000-100,000 (depends on regrowth strategy)
+## CRITICAL ISSUE: ISSUES EXPLODING (Week 22)
+- Open issues: 3,319 (was 1,246 → nearly tripled!)
+- This week: 4,500 new issues vs 1,707 resolved = NET +2,793
+- Outage (90 min) this week created massive spike in new issues
+- At $400/day ops: resolved 244/day (insufficient)
+- INCREASED ops to $800/day total to address backlog
+- Expected resolution at $800/day: ~350-400/day
+- Normal issue creation (no outage): ~300-350/day
+- Should stabilize issues if NO MORE OUTAGES
+- Issue age: 1,861 new(0-2d), 1,790 recent(3-7d), 152 old(8-14d), 236 ancient(14d+)
+
+## CHURN ANALYSIS (Week 22)
+- Total cancellations: 640 (UP from 363!)
+- 546 after 1st billing, 94 after 2nd+ billing
+- NO pre-billing churn this week (all churners had paid at least once)
+- Major driver: billing wave + issue dissatisfaction + outage
+- Social media full of support complaint posts ("radio silence", "ghosted")
+
+## Financial Breakdown (Week 22 Actual)
+- Revenue: $14,420 (subscription payments)
+- Costs: $13,058 (capacity $3,710 + compute $4,285 + ops $2,800 + dev $1,050 + ads $700 + leads $513)
+- Net: +$1,362 (positive! but barely)
+- With NEW ops at $800/day: costs increase by $2,800/week
+- NEW expected weekly costs: ~$14,800-15,200
+- Expected revenue: ~$13,500-14,500 (declining with fewer subs)
+- NEW expected net: -$300 to -$1,700/week (slight cash drain)
+
+## PAYING SUBSCRIBER BREAKDOWN
+- Plan A @ $14/mo: 2,669 subs (main revenue driver)
+- Plan A @ $12/mo: 356 subs (legacy pricing)
+- Plan A @ $10/mo: 789 subs (legacy pricing)
+- Plan A @ $7/mo: 92 subs (new promo cohort)
+- Plan A @ $15/mo: 3 subs
+- Plan B @ $49/mo: 124 subs
+- Total monthly revenue from payers: $56,352 → $13,149/week base
+
+## Free Period Subs Still in Pipeline
+- Free period: 1,341 Plan A + 160 Plan B + 1 Plan C = 1,502
+- These will all hit billing over next 3-4 weeks
+- Expected 40-50% conversion rate → ~600-750 new paying subs
+- This OFFSETS some of the paying churn
+
+## Economy & Market
+- PMI: 45.3 (unchanged, contraction, at trough)
+- Enterprise: frozen (0 threads, 0 activity)
+- Social media posts mention "PMI 48.8" in some contexts - forward looking?
+
+## Quality Status (Day 154)
+- S1 Plan A (T2): 0.5727 (global bonus 0.2066, S1 bonus 0.3570)
+- Quality growth: minimal with $150/day dev
+- Competitor pressure: ~0.04-0.06/week expectation increases
+- Gap widening slowly - acceptable for survival period
+
+## Social Media Strategy
+- Posted concrete support update (promising 48hr response time)
+- Previous vague post got negative backlash ("coming soon" not good enough)
+- Users complain about: support ghosting, usage caps, lag spikes
+- LESSON: Be specific in social posts, not vague promises
+
+## Cash Runway Projections (from Day 154)
+- Week 23 (1wk): ~$46,000 (slight negative)
+- Week 25 (4wk): ~$43,000 (gradual decline during billing wave)
+- Week 33 (12wk): ~$40,000-45,000 (stabilization after billing wave)
+- Week 47 (26wk): ~$35,000-50,000 (depends on growth restart)
+
+## Strategy: SURVIVE THE ISSUE CRISIS
+- Phase 1 (Weeks 22-25): Higher ops to resolve issues, accept slight cash drain
+- Phase 2 (Weeks 26-28): Issues resolved, reduce ops, return to cash positive
+- Phase 3 (Weeks 28+): Stabilize at break-even, consider growth restart
+- KEY: Must prevent outages (ops reduces probability)
 
 ## Key Risks (RANKED)
-1. **Issues growing (1,246 → higher with ops cuts)** - could accelerate churn
-2. **Quality gap widening** (competitor pressure vs our minimal dev)
-3. **Lead promo change ($14→$7)** - might kill new sub acquisition
-4. **Economy PMI 46.9** - enterprise frozen
-5. **Subscriber count declining** - fewer subs = less revenue over time
-
-## Strategy: SURVIVE THEN GROW
-- Phase 1 (Weeks 21-25): Extreme cost cuts, accumulate cash, survive billing wave
-- Phase 2 (Weeks 26-30): Stabilize when churn normalizes, cautiously reinvest
-- Phase 3 (Weeks 30+): Resume growth spending when cash > $70K
-- R&D restart: When cash > $167K (far future, maybe week 60+)
-
-## Social Media Post (Week 21)
-- Posted acknowledgment of user feedback, promising improvements
-- Hope it helps reputation
+1. **Another outage** - would create 2000+ new issues, accelerate churn
+2. **Issues not resolving fast enough** - 3,319 backlog damaging satisfaction
+3. **Revenue decline** - fewer paying subs as legacy users churn
+4. **Quality gap** - competitors advancing while we're in survival mode
+5. **PMI 45.3** - no enterprise recovery in sight
 
 ## Mistakes to Avoid (VERIFIED)
-- DON'T increase quotas without checking compute cost impact!
-- Lead promo $14 = free first month (creates compute waste + first-billing churn wave)
-- Content marketing most cost-effective channel for S1
-- Issues scale with subscribers - ops must scale too
 - subscriptions: status='subscribed' (not 'active'), 'cancelled', 'lost'
-- Use start_day in subscriptions, end_day for cancellation timing
-- Enterprise turns: closed=0 for open threads
-- R&D boosts add to global_bonus
-- Competitor events permanently raise expectations
-- PMI 46.9 (contraction, slightly improved)
-- $7 lead promo = new subs pay $7 first month (not free)
-
-## Next Week Priorities (Week 22)
-1. CHECK if cost cuts are working (burn should be <$1K or positive)
-2. Monitor issue count - if >1,500, may need to bump ops back up
-3. Watch conversion rate with $7 promo (was 27% with $14 promo)
-4. Revenue should be $14,000-16,000 (stable)
-5. Churn should start declining as billing wave passes
-6. If cash grows to $48K+, strategy is working - hold steady
+- issues: open_day, days_open, status, resolved_day columns
+- ledger: category column (not type)
+- DON'T set ops too low ($400 was insufficient - issues tripled!)
+- $800/day ops is minimum to keep up with issue creation (without outages)
+- Content marketing most cost-effective channel for S1
+- Social posts: be SPECIFIC and concrete, not vague promises
+- R&D cheapest tier: $167K (far from our $47K cash)
+- Outage at $400 ops: ~3%/day. At $700 ops: ~1%/day. Must keep ops up.
+
+## Next Week Priorities (Week 23)
+1. Monitor issue count - MUST start declining (target <3,000)
+2. Monitor churn - should slow down from 640 peak
+3. Revenue should be $13,000-14,500
+4. Cash should be $45,000-47,000
+5. If issues declining → strategy working, hold steady
+6. If issues still growing → may need to further increase ops (cuts elsewhere)
+7. NO social post this week (already posted, max 1/week)
